data.title: AI TRENDS | Xiaomi Launches MiMo-V2-Pro AI Model with Competitive Pricing
data.description: Xiaomi has introduced its latest AI model, MiMo-V2-Pro, on March 18. According to NS3.AI, this model features over one trillion parameters and is priced at $1 per million input tokens and $3 per million output tokens. MiMo-V2-Pro has achieved an eighth-place ranking on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and scored 78% on the SWE-bench Verified. While hands-on tests have shown strong performance in coding and creative writing, the model exhibits weaker capabilities in frontier-math tasks.

data.title: Aave Governance Dispute Intensifies Amid v4 Upgrade Preparations
data.description: Aave is currently experiencing a governance dispute concerning the allocation of product revenue back to the DAO as it gears up for its v4 upgrade. According to NS3.AI, the conflict escalated following Aave Labs' proposal of "Aave Will Win" in February. The situation further intensified when the governance group ACI announced in early March that it would cease operations due to disagreements with the developer firm.

data.title: Expert Warns of Risks in Outsourcing Bitcoin Custody
data.description: Kevin Loaec has highlighted potential risks for institutions outsourcing Bitcoin custody. According to NS3.AI, Loaec pointed out that relying on pooled custody, capped or conditional insurance, and vendor dependence could expose clients to systemic failures. He emphasized that policy-driven wallets could enforce governance on-chain, potentially mitigating some of these risks.

data.title: El Salvador's Bitcoin reserves have increased to 7,605.37, worth approximately $506 million.
data.description: El Salvador's official agency, The Bitcoin Office, announced on the X platform that the country's strategic Bitcoin reserve holdings have increased to 7,605.37 Bitcoins, worth approximately $506 million.

data.title: El Salvador's Bitcoin Reserves Reach New Milestone
data.description: El Salvador's official entity, The Bitcoin Office, announced on the X platform that the country's Bitcoin strategic reserves have surpassed 7,600 BTC. According to ChainCatcher, the reserves have increased to 7,605.37 BTC. The value of these holdings is approximately $506 million.

data.title: Ethereum's Market Position Threatened by Stablecoin Growth
data.description:
Ether's (ETH) dominance as the second-largest cryptocurrency is under threat, not due to its proximity to Bitcoin (BTC), but because of the rapid expansion of the stablecoin market. According to Cointelegraph, Ether's position is being challenged as Tether (USDT) and other stablecoins experience significant growth. Over the past five years, Ether has lagged behind top stablecoins like USDT and USD Coin (USDC) in terms of market capitalization growth. While ETH's market cap increased by approximately 11.75% to around $240 billion, USDT saw a staggering 622.50% growth, reaching over $184 billion. This trend has led to speculation about Ethereum's potential loss of its number-two spot by 2026.

On platforms like Polymarket, a significant number of traders are betting on Ethereum being overtaken by 2026, with over 59% of participants predicting this outcome, a notable increase from 17% at the start of the year. The differing growth trajectories of Ethereum and Tether can be attributed to their distinct natures; Ethereum's value is heavily reliant on the price of ETH, which has struggled amid macroeconomic pressures such as U.S. tariffs, geopolitical tensions, and shifting Federal Reserve policies. This has also impacted institutional interest, with U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs experiencing a 65% drop in assets under management, falling to $11.76 billion in March from $31.86 billion in October of the previous year.

In contrast, Tether's growth is fueled by capital inflows into stablecoins, which are seen as safer, more liquid alternatives during volatile market conditions. The total stablecoin market has surged to $310 billion from just $5 billion in 2020, with Tether holding a 58% share. This "dry powder" effect, where capital is parked in dollar-pegged assets, remains strong during risk-off periods. For Ethereum to regain momentum, a stronger risk appetite is necessary to boost ETH's price, whereas Tether benefits from investor caution. This dynamic explains why ETH's market cap growth has lagged behind USDT, despite Ethereum's foundational role in the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

Looking ahead to 2026, Ether faces potential price declines from a technical standpoint. As of Sunday, ETH was trading within a "bear flag" pattern, which suggests a higher likelihood of further downward movement if the price breaks below the pattern's lower trendline. Should this occur, ETH's price could fall towards the pattern's downside target of approximately $1,250 by June, assuming the breakdown continues.

data.title: Trump's Ultimatum to Iran May Increase Oil Prices and Market Volatility
data.description: Garret Jin, the agent for the '1011 Insider Whale,' indicated that U.S. President Donald Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran regarding passage through the Strait of Hormuz could lead to higher oil prices and increased market volatility. According to NS3.AI, Jin noted that the crude oil volatility index OVX has risen to 93, while the CBOE Volatility Index VIX is approximately 24, resulting in a ratio close to four times.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Cathie Wood Highlights AI's Potential in Healthcare
data.description: Cathie Wood, founder of Ark Invest, emphasized the underestimated potential of artificial intelligence in healthcare during a recent interview. According to PANews, Wood believes AI can revolutionize cancer diagnosis through blood tests before symptoms appear. She also noted that the pharmaceutical industry faces a $300 billion patent cliff over the next five years, presenting both challenges and opportunities.

data.title: Crypto Asset Outflows from South Korea Reach $60 Billion
data.description: In a significant development in the Asian cryptocurrency market, $60 billion in crypto assets have been reported as outflows from South Korea. According to NS3.AI, this movement highlights the ongoing shifts in the region's digital asset landscape. Additionally, Russia has introduced new listing standards for cryptocurrency exchanges, aiming to regulate and stabilize its crypto market. Meanwhile, Circle and Tether have taken action by freezing addresses associated with Wallex, reflecting heightened scrutiny and regulatory measures in the industry.

data.title: Polygon Network Achieves Record Stablecoin Transactions
data.description: Polygon founder Sandeep Nailwal announced on the X platform that the Polygon network recorded 159.9 million stablecoin transactions last week, setting a new all-time high. According to PANews, recent data also shows an increase in stablecoin transaction volume, fee burn rate, and the number of active addresses on the network.

data.title: Trump Issues Ultimatum to Iran Over Strait of Hormuz Passage
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran demanding the full restoration of passage through the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, this development marks a significant escalation in tensions. Garrett Jin, an agent for '1011 Insider Whale,' shared on the X platform that this move could have three major impacts.

Firstly, diplomatic buffers may be broken, potentially ending the discreet negotiations between Iran and Japan regarding the passage of ships. Secondly, the nature of the conflict could escalate, especially if attacks extend to civilian infrastructure like electricity, which could strengthen Iran's stance and increase the likelihood of a prolonged conflict.

Lastly, volatility in the oil market is expected to surge, with the oil volatility index (OVX) rising to 93, compared to the Chicago Board Options Exchange Volatility Index (VIX) at approximately 24. This ratio, nearing four times, indicates that the risk in the oil market far exceeds stock market pricing. Overall, oil prices and market volatility are likely to continue rising, with expectations for a prolonged conflict duration.

data.title: Iran Monitors U.S. Aircraft Carrier Lincoln's Movements
data.description: On March 29, Iranian Navy Commander Shahram Irani stated that Iran is closely monitoring the position and movements of the U.S. aircraft carrier Lincoln and its requests to regional countries. According to BlockBeats, Iran has indicated that it will launch missiles if the Lincoln carrier group enters its range.

data.title: Agent of "1011 Insider Whale": Geopolitical conflicts may drive up oil prices and market volatility; contingency plans need to be developed in advance.
data.description: Garrett Jin, an agent for "1011 Insider Whale," published an article on the X platform stating that Trump has issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran, demanding the full resumption of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a significant escalation of the situation. He summarized this move as having three key impacts: First, the diplomatic buffer has been broken, potentially ending the previous low-key negotiation path between Iran and Japan regarding the passage of ships one by one; second, the nature of the conflict has escalated, and if the attack expands to civilian infrastructure such as power plants, it will strengthen Iran's position and increase the probability of a protracted war; third, oil market volatility has surged, and market mismatch has intensified, with the crude oil volatility index (OVX) rising to 93, while the Chicago Board Options Exchange Volatility Index (VIX) is around 24, a ratio of nearly 4, indicating that the risks in the oil market far exceed those priced into the stock market. Overall, oil prices and market volatility may continue to rise, and the duration of the conflict is expected to be prolonged.

data.title: Australia's New Law Faces Challenges in Restricting Under-16s on Social Media
data.description: Australia's recent legislation aimed at preventing under-16s from accessing social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok is showing mixed results. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while some parents are seeing positive outcomes, many teenagers are discovering ways to circumvent the restrictions. The law, designed to protect young users from potential online harm, has been met with both praise and criticism. Parents have reported a decrease in their children's screen time and exposure to inappropriate content. However, the adaptability of tech-savvy teens poses a challenge to the law's effectiveness. Experts suggest that ongoing adjustments and monitoring will be necessary to ensure the legislation achieves its intended goals. The situation underscores the complexities of regulating digital spaces and the need for continuous innovation in policy-making.

data.title: Iran says it is monitoring the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier in real time and will launch missiles once it enters its range.
data.description: Iranian Navy Commander Shahram Irani stated on the 29th local time that Iran is monitoring the location and movements of the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group in real time, as well as its requests to countries in the region. Iran will launch missiles if the Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group enters its firing range. (CCTV News)

data.title: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 Market Reaches Record Volume Amid Growing Onchain Demand
data.description: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 market achieved a new milestone on March 23, recording approximately $5.4 billion in perpetual futures volume across commodities and macro assets. According to NS3.AI, this surge indicates an increasing demand for onchain macro exposure. However, industry participants note that limited liquidity continues to hinder these venues from matching the scale of traditional markets.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Brain Aurora Partners with Tokyo Lifestyle for AI Health Platform
data.description: Brain Aurora (6681) has announced a strategic partnership with Tokyo Lifestyle Co., Ltd. (TKLF) to develop an integrated platform focusing on digital therapeutics for cognitive disorders and functional health products. According to Ming Pao, the collaboration aims to explore the combination of online cognitive assessment and training with offline functional health products in regions such as Japan and Southeast Asia. The initiative is dedicated to creating an AI-driven digital health platform for the elderly.

data.title: Viewpoint: Key resistance levels for ETH are at $2029 and $2079.
data.description: Analyst Ali Charts stated that Ethereum's current key resistance levels are at $2029 and $2079; the support levels are at $1881, $1584, and $1238.

data.title: Ethereum's Key Support and Resistance Levels Identified by Analyst
data.description: Ethereum's current support levels are identified at $1,881, $1,584, and $1,238, with resistance levels at $2,029 and $2,079. According to BlockBeats, these insights were provided by analyst @alicharts on March 29.
